tickerText: [TENNIS] HSBC Championships (London): Donna Vekic defeats Emma Raducanu (6-0, 7-6) to win the women's title; men's ATP 500 event underway with De Minaur, Norrie, and Lehecka in second-round action • Boss Open (Stuttgart): Ben Shelton defeats Taylor Fritz (6-4, 2-6, 6-4) for his first grass-court title • [BADMINTON] Sathio Group Australian Open (Sydney): Akane Yamaguchi wins women's singles; Alwi Farhan claims men's title as the Super 500 concludes (June 14) • [SQUASH] PSA Squash Tour Finals (Paris, June 17–20): World champions Mostafa Asal and Amina Orfi top the draws at Centquatre-Paris; Olivia Weaver withdraws, Tinne Gilis steps in • [PICKLEBALL] PPA Australia 125 Wellington: Mitchell Hargreaves and Andie Dikosavljevic sweep all four pro titles; PPA Asia 500 China Open begins June 17 in Beijing • [TABLE TENNIS] Kanak Jha jumps to world No. 24 after WTT Contender Skopje; WTT Star Contender Slovenia underway; US Smash set for Los Angeles June 26

newsItems.title: Vekic Dominates Raducanu for Queen's Title; Shelton Wins Stuttgart
newsItems.excerpt: Donna Vekic captured the HSBC Championships women's crown with a commanding 6-0, 7-6(6) victory over Emma Raducanu at Queen's Club. Meanwhile, Ben Shelton edged Taylor Fritz (6-4, 2-6, 6-4) in Stuttgart to claim his first-ever grass-court ATP title ahead of Wimbledon.

newsItems.title: Yamaguchi & Farhan Crowned at Australian Badminton Open
newsItems.excerpt: The Sathio Group Australian Open concluded in Sydney with Akane Yamaguchi defeating Pornpawee Chochuwong in the women's final and Indonesia's Alwi Farhan taking men's singles gold. Chinese pairs swept the doubles events at the Super 500 tournament.

newsItems.title: PSA Squash Tour Finals Draw Set for Historic Paris Debut
newsItems.excerpt: The season-ending PSA Squash Tour Finals begin June 17 at Centquatre-Paris, with world champions Mostafa Asal and Amina Orfi leading the fields. Olivia Weaver and Youssef Ibrahim have withdrawn due to injury, with Tinne Gilis and Marwan ElShorbagy called up as replacements.

newsItems.title: Kanak Jha Rises to World No. 24 Ahead of US Smash
newsItems.excerpt: American Kanak Jha climbed six spots to world No. 24 in the latest ITTF rankings following his runner-up finish at WTT Contender Skopje. The WTT Star Contender in Slovenia is underway, with the star-studded US Smash in Los Angeles set for June 26.
